mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market momentum that increases consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 139,004k as of July 2014 shows continued post-crisis employment recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS momentum signal falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months fail to produce positive 20-day returns in >55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero levels (below 0.25%) signals an extended monetary accommodation regime that suppresses equity risk premiums and sustains equity valuations, producing positive forward returns over 60 trading days as long as rates remain pinned.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of July 2014 indicates continued zero-bound policy
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during zero-bound periods show negative mean return or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year window. Hypothesis rejected if FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25% and SPX continues to rally without reversal within 60 days.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with accelerating housing starts (HOUST) indicates strengthening consumer demand and construction activity that boosts corporate revenues and equity valuations. The joint signal is stronger than either component alone, predicting positive 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 439,802M and HOUST at 893k units as of June-July 2014
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST growth conditions are met simultaneously, 20-day forward returns are negative in >45% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term is <0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness or demand destruction that reduces corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO months produce positive 5-day returns in >50% of instances, or Rank IC of the signal is <0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 6.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, triggering a negative equity repricing over 20 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.2% as of July 2014, below trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of instances, or Rank IC of the signal is <0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year combined with the Federal Reserve maintaining FEDFUNDS above 1.0% signals monetary policy tightening that compresses equity valuations and increases discount rates, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.69 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of June-July 2014
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: When both inflation and rates conditions are met, 60-day forward returns are positive in >50% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term is <0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that increases corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive 60-day forward returns as the expansion narrative strengthens market sentiment.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15985.7B (chained 2017 dollars) as of Q2 2014
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth >2.5% produce negative 60-day forward returns in >45% of instances, or Rank IC of the signal is <0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerating month-over-month above 0.4% combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals synchronized supply and labor expansion that increases productive capacity and corporate output, driving positive 20-day equity returns as growth expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.4% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.1%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.39 and PAYEMS at 139,004k as of July 2014
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and PAYEMS growth conditions are met, 20-day forward returns are negative in >48% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term is <0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
